title: National Academies

1970 | [Medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine") | [Victor Dzau](/wiki/Victor_Dzau "Victor Dzau") | Established as the Institute of Medicine (1970–2015)

[England](/wiki/England "England") | 1660 | [Hard sciences](/wiki/Hard_sciences "Hard sciences"), [soft sciences](/wiki/Soft_sciences "Soft sciences") | [Charles III](/wiki/Charles_III "Charles III")[[5]](#cite_note-5) | The Invisible College was a precursor to the Royal Society of London. In 1660, the informal committee of 12 philosophers formed the College for the Promoting of Physico-Mathematical Experimental Learning.

[Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") | 1783 | [Hard sciences](/wiki/Hard_sciences "Hard sciences"), [soft sciences](/wiki/Soft_sciences "Soft sciences"), [arts](/wiki/Arts "Arts"), [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities"), [medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine"), [social sciences](/wiki/Social_sciences "Social sciences") | [Sir Anton Muscatelli](/wiki/Sir_Anton_Muscatelli "Sir Anton Muscatelli") as President | The Royal Society was founded during the [Scottish Enlightenment](/wiki/Scottish_Enlightenment "Scottish Enlightenment") as the Edinburgh Society for Improving Arts and Sciences.

[England](/wiki/England "England") | 1902 | [Humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities") and [social sciences](/wiki/Social_sciences "Social sciences") | [Professor Julia Black CBE FBA](https://www.thebritishacademy.ac.uk/fellows/julia-black-pba/) as President | The British Academy was first proposed in 1899 as the British Academy for the Promotion of Historical, Philosophical and Philological Studies. The name was subsequently shortened and incorporated in 1901, receiving [royal charter](/wiki/Royal_charter "Royal charter") from [King Edward VII](/wiki/King_Edward_VII "King Edward VII") in 1902.

[England](/wiki/England "England") | 1976 | [Engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering") | [Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh](/wiki/Prince_Philip,_Duke_of_Edinburgh "Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh") was Senior Fellow until his death[[6]](#cite_note-6)  [Anne, Princess Royal](/wiki/Anne,_Princess_Royal "Anne, Princess Royal") and [Prince Edward, Duke of Kent](/wiki/Prince_Edward,_Duke_of_Kent "Prince Edward, Duke of Kent") as Royal Fellows[[7]](#cite_note-7)  [Sir James McDonald](/wiki/Jim_McDonald_(electrical_engineer) "Jim McDonald (electrical engineer)") as President[[8]](#cite_note-8) | The Fellowship of Engineering was conceived in the late 1960s under [Harold Wilson](/wiki/Harold_Wilson "Harold Wilson"), and subsequently established in 1976. It was granted [royal charter](/wiki/Royal_charter "Royal charter") in 1983 and renamed the Royal Academy of Engineering in 1992.

[England](/wiki/England "England") | 1998 | [Biomedical](/wiki/Biomedical "Biomedical") and [health research](/wiki/Health_research "Health research") | [Professor Dame Anne Johnson](/wiki/Anne_Mandall_Johnson "Anne Mandall Johnson") as President | The academy was established by a working group chaired by [Michael Atiyah](/wiki/Michael_Atiyah "Michael Atiyah").

is an organizational body, usually operating with [state](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)") financial support and approval, that co-ordinates scholarly [research](/wiki/Research "Research") activities and standards for [academic disciplines](/wiki/Academic_disciplines "Academic disciplines"), and serves as [public policy](/wiki/Public_policy "Public policy") advisors, [research institutes](/wiki/Research_institute "Research institute"), [think tanks](/wiki/Think_tank "Think tank"), and [public administration](/wiki/Public_administration "Public administration") [consultants](/wiki/Consultant "Consultant") for governments or on issues of public importance, most frequently in the [sciences](/wiki/Science "Science") but also in the [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities"). Typically the country's [learned societies](/wiki/Learned_society "Learned society") in individual disciplines will liaise with or be coordinated by the national academy. National academies play an important organisational role in academic exchanges and collaborations between countries.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [National Academies](/w/index.php?title=National_Academies&redirect=no "National Academies"))

Organizational body

A **national academy** is an organizational body, usually operating with [state](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)") financial support and approval, that co-ordinates scholarly [research](/wiki/Research "Research") activities and standards for [academic disciplines](/wiki/Academic_disciplines "Academic disciplines"), and serves as [public policy](/wiki/Public_policy "Public policy") advisors, [research institutes](/wiki/Research_institute "Research institute"), [think tanks](/wiki/Think_tank "Think tank"), and [public administration](/wiki/Public_administration "Public administration") [consultants](/wiki/Consultant "Consultant") for governments or on issues of public importance, most frequently in the [sciences](/wiki/Science "Science") but also in the [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities"). Typically the country's [learned societies](/wiki/Learned_society "Learned society") in individual disciplines will liaise with or be coordinated by the national academy. National academies play an important organisational role in academic exchanges and collaborations between countries.

The extent of official recognition of national academies varies between countries. In some cases they are explicitly or de facto an arm of government; in others, as in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), they are voluntary, [non-profit](/wiki/Non-profit "Non-profit") bodies with which the government has agreed to negotiate, and which may receive government financial support while retaining substantial independence. In some countries, a single academy covers all disciplines. In others, there are several academies, which work together more or less closely; for example, [France](/wiki/France "France"), where the [Institut de France](/wiki/Institut_de_France "Institut de France") groups five self-governing Academies, or [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). In many states, they are organized in [academies of science](/wiki/Academy_of_Sciences "Academy of Sciences"). In the countries of the former [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union"), and in the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China"), the national academies have considerable power over policy and personnel in their areas. There is, however, a growing consensus among international federations of learned academies that bona fide national (or learned) academies need to adhere to certain criteria:

* The [fellowship](/wiki/Fellowship "Fellowship") is elected, on the basis of excellence, by existing fellows (members)
* The number of fellows is restricted either to a total number or to a rate of accretion
* The governance of the academy is democratic and "bottom up". The fellowship is the ultimate source of the academy's authority
* The academy is independent of government, industry and professional associations. Most, if not all, academies derive some financial support from some or all of these other organisations but this support needs to be given in a manner that does not compromise the academy's independence.

## United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_academy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: United States")]

In 1863, [President of the United States](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") [Abraham Lincoln](/wiki/Abraham_Lincoln "Abraham Lincoln") incorporated the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") [National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine](/wiki/National_Academies_of_Sciences,_Engineering,_and_Medicine "National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine") (NASEM). The affiliated organizations were granted congressional charters to operate under the National Academy of Sciences. In 1916 President [Woodrow Wilson](/wiki/Woodrow_Wilson "Woodrow Wilson") reincorporated the organization under the [National Research Council](/wiki/National_Research_Council_(United_States) "National Research Council (United States)") to foster scientific research emphasizing American industries. Today NASEM is composed of three [non-profit](/wiki/Non-profit "Non-profit") member organizations: the National Academy of Sciences (NAS), the National Academy of Engineering (NAE), and the [National Academy of Medicine (NAM)](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Medicine "National Academy of Medicine") (after 2015; formerly Institute of Medicine (IoM)). The U.S. national academies also serve as [public policy](/wiki/Public_policy "Public policy") advisors, [research institutes](/wiki/Research_institute "Research institute"), [think tanks](/wiki/Think_tank "Think tank"), and [public administration](/wiki/Public_administration "Public administration") [consultants](/wiki/Consultant "Consultant") on issues of public importance or on request by the government.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

| Name | Year founded | Area of focus | President | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [National Academy of Sciences](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Sciences "National Academy of Sciences") | 1863 | [Science](/wiki/Science "Science") | [Marcia McNutt](/wiki/Marcia_McNutt "Marcia McNutt") |  |
| [National Academy of Engineering](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Engineering "National Academy of Engineering") | 1964 | [Engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering") | [John L. Anderson](/wiki/John_L._Anderson "John L. Anderson") |  |
| [National Academy of Medicine](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Medicine "National Academy of Medicine") | 1970 | [Medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine") | [Victor Dzau](/wiki/Victor_Dzau "Victor Dzau") | Established as the Institute of Medicine (1970–2015) |

## United Kingdom

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_academy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: United Kingdom")]

In the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") four national academies are the major [learned societies](/wiki/Learned_societies "Learned societies") of England: the [Academy of Medical Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Medical_Sciences "Academy of Medical Sciences"), [British Academy](/wiki/British_Academy "British Academy"), the [Royal Academy of Engineering](/wiki/Royal_Academy_of_Engineering "Royal Academy of Engineering") and the [Royal Society](/wiki/Royal_Society "Royal Society").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In addition, there are the [Learned Society of Wales](/wiki/Learned_Society_of_Wales "Learned Society of Wales") in [Wales](/wiki/Wales "Wales") and the [Royal Society of Edinburgh](/wiki/Royal_Society_of_Edinburgh "Royal Society of Edinburgh") in [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"). The [UK Young Academy](/wiki/UK_Young_Academy "UK Young Academy") is for young scientists.

The name was subsequently shortened and incorporated in 1901, receiving [royal charter](/wiki/Royal_charter "Royal charter") from [King Edward VII](/wiki/King_Edward_VII "King Edward VII") in 1902. |
| [Royal Academy of Engineering](/wiki/Royal_Academy_of_Engineering "Royal Academy of Engineering") | [England](/wiki/England "England") | 1976 | [Engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering") | [Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh](/wiki/Prince_Philip,_Duke_of_Edinburgh "Prince Philip, Duke of Edinburgh") was Senior Fellow until his death[[6]](#cite_note-6)  [Anne, Princess Royal](/wiki/Anne,_Princess_Royal "Anne, Princess Royal") and [Prince Edward, Duke of Kent](/wiki/Prince_Edward,_Duke_of_Kent "Prince Edward, Duke of Kent") as Royal Fellows[[7]](#cite_note-7)  [Sir James McDonald](/wiki/Jim_McDonald_(electrical_engineer) "Jim McDonald (electrical engineer)") as President[[8]](#cite_note-8) | The Fellowship of Engineering was conceived in the late 1960s under [Harold Wilson](/wiki/Harold_Wilson "Harold Wilson"), and subsequently established in 1976. It was granted [royal charter](/wiki/Royal_charter "Royal charter") in 1983 and renamed the Royal Academy of Engineering in 1992. |
| [Academy of Medical Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Medical_Sciences "Academy of Medical Sciences") | [England](/wiki/England "England") | 1998 | [Biomedical](/wiki/Biomedical "Biomedical") and [health research](/wiki/Health_research "Health research") | [Professor Dame Anne Johnson](/wiki/Anne_Mandall_Johnson "Anne Mandall Johnson") as President | The academy was established by a working group chaired by [Michael Atiyah](/wiki/Michael_Atiyah "Michael Atiyah"). |

Thomas](/wiki/Hywel_R._Thomas "Hywel R. Thomas") as President;  Charles, [Prince of Wales](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales "Prince of Wales") as Patron |  |

## Japan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_academy&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Japan")]

In Japan, all of the national academies were established during the early years of the [Showa Era](/wiki/Sh%C5%8Dwa_era "Shōwa era"). The two premier national academies in the country are the [Science Council of Japan](/wiki/Science_Council_of_Japan "Science Council of Japan") and [The Japan Academy](/wiki/Japan_Academy "Japan Academy"). Representing the artistic profession and literature is the [Japan Art Academy](/wiki/Japan_Art_Academy "Japan Art Academy").

The [Science Council of Japan](/wiki/Science_Council_of_Japan "Science Council of Japan") (SCJ) was founded by American [physicist](/wiki/Physicist "Physicist") [Harry C. Kelly](/wiki/Harry_C._Kelly "Harry C. Kelly") in 1949 during the [Allied occupation of Japan](/wiki/Occupation_of_Japan "Occupation of Japan") as a special organisation under the [Prime Minister's](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Japan "Prime Minister of Japan") jurisdiction, operating independently from the [Government of Japan](/wiki/Government_of_Japan "Government of Japan") to promoting and enhancing scientific research in the country. The SCJ represents all Japanese scientists, researchers and engineers from multiple fields from the natural sciences to the humanities, making necessary policy recommendations relating to science and technology to the national government. The SCJ has 210 Council Members stipulated by a mandated quota as well as 2,000 Associate Members nationwide.

| Name | Year founded | Area of focus | President | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Science Council of Japan](/wiki/Science_Council_of_Japan "Science Council of Japan") 日本学術会議 | 1949 | [Social sciences](/wiki/Social_science "Social science"), [natural sciences](/wiki/Natural_science "Natural science"), [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering"), [medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine"), [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities") | Takaaki Kajita |  |
| [The Japan Academy](/wiki/Japan_Academy "Japan Academy") 日本学士院 | 1947 | [Social sciences](/wiki/Social_science "Social science"), [natural sciences](/wiki/Natural_science "Natural science"), [engineering](/wiki/Engineering "Engineering"), [medicine](/wiki/Medicine "Medicine"), [humanities](/wiki/Humanities "Humanities") | Takeshi Sasaki | The Japan Academy was originally established as the Tokyo Academy, becoming the Imperial Academy later in 1907. The Academy awards three prestigious awards: The Imperial Prize, The Duke of Edinburgh Prize, and The Japan Academy Prize. |
| [Japan Art Academy](/wiki/Japan_Art_Academy "Japan Art Academy") 日本芸術院 | 1937 | [Fine art](/wiki/Fine_art "Fine art"), [literature](/wiki/Literature "Literature"), [music](/wiki/Music "Music"), [drama](/wiki/Drama "Drama"), dance | Man Nomura |  |

These are government-funded, but self-governing.
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Flag_of_San_Marino.svg/20px-Flag_of_San_Marino.svg.png) [San Marino](/wiki/San_Marino "San Marino"): [International Academy of Sciences San Marino](/wiki/International_Academy_of_Sciences_San_Marino "International Academy of Sciences San Marino")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/10/Flag_of_Scotland.svg/40px-Flag_of_Scotland.svg.png) [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"): [Royal Society of Edinburgh](/wiki/Royal_Society_of_Edinburgh "Royal Society of Edinburgh")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/ff/Flag_of_Serbia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Serbia.svg.png) [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia"): [Serbian Academy of Sciences and Arts](/wiki/Serbian_Academy_of_Sciences_and_Arts "Serbian Academy of Sciences and Arts")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Flag_of_Slovenia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Slovenia.svg.png) [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"): [Slovenian Academy of Sciences and Arts](/wiki/Slovenian_Academy_of_Sciences_and_Arts "Slovenian Academy of Sciences and Arts")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/Flag_of_South_Africa.svg/40px-Flag_of_South_Africa.svg.png) [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa"): [Academy of Science of South Africa](/wiki/Academy_of_Science_of_South_Africa "Academy of Science of South Africa")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/9/9a/Flag_of_Spain.svg/40px-Flag_of_Spain.svg.png) [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"): The [Royal Academy](/wiki/Real_Academia_Espa%C3%B1ola "Real Academia Española") is the main reference body for the [Spanish language](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"). [Real Academia de Bellas Artes de San Fernando](/wiki/Real_Academia_de_Bellas_Artes_de_San_Fernando "Real Academia de Bellas Artes de San Fernando") covers the artistic fields; [Real Academia de la Historia](/wiki/Real_Academia_de_la_Historia "Real Academia de la Historia") is the organising body for History; the Royal Academy of Moral and Political Sciences covers the humanities; [Royal Academy of Exact, Physical and Natural Sciences](/wiki/Real_Academia_de_Ciencias_Exactas,_F%C3%ADsicas_y_Naturales "Real Academia de Ciencias Exactas, Físicas y Naturales") covers natural sciences and mathematics; [Real Academia de Jurisprudencia y Legislación](/wiki/Real_Academia_de_Jurisprudencia_y_Legislaci%C3%B3n "Real Academia de Jurisprudencia y Legislación") covers the field of law.
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/11/Flag_of_Sri_Lanka.svg/40px-Flag_of_Sri_Lanka.svg.png) [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka"): [National Academy of Sciences of Sri Lanka](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Sciences_of_Sri_Lanka "National Academy of Sciences of Sri Lanka")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/01/Flag_of_Sudan.svg/40px-Flag_of_Sudan.svg.png) [Sudan](/wiki/Sudan "Sudan"): [Sudanese National Academy of Sciences](/wiki/Sudanese_National_Academy_of_Sciences "Sudanese National Academy of Sciences")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4c/Flag_of_Sweden.svg/40px-Flag_of_Sweden.svg.png) [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"): [Swedish Academy](/wiki/Swedish_Academy "Swedish Academy") for language, [Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences](/wiki/Royal_Swedish_Academy_of_Sciences "Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences") and the [Royal Swedish Academy of Engineering Sciences](/wiki/Royal_Swedish_Academy_of_Engineering_Sciences "Royal Swedish Academy of Engineering Sciences")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/72/Flag_of_the_Republic_of_China.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_Republic_of_China.svg.png) [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") (Republic of China): [Academia Sinica](/wiki/Academia_Sinica "Academia Sinica")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Flag_of_Thailand.svg/40px-Flag_of_Thailand.svg.png) [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"): [Royal Institute of Thailand](/wiki/Royal_Institute_of_Thailand "Royal Institute of Thailand")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b4/Flag_of_Turkey.svg/40px-Flag_of_Turkey.svg.png) [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"): [Turkish Academy of Sciences](/wiki/Turkish_Academy_of_Sciences "Turkish Academy of Sciences") is funded by the government but maintains autonomy
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/Flag_of_Ukraine.svg/40px-Flag_of_Ukraine.svg.png) [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"): [National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Sciences_of_Ukraine "National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine"). Other state organizations also include [National Academy of Arts of Ukraine](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Arts_of_Ukraine "National Academy of Arts of Ukraine"), [National Academy of Medical Sciences of Ukraine](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Medical_Sciences_of_Ukraine "National Academy of Medical Sciences of Ukraine"), [National Academy of Agrarian Sciences of Ukraine](/w/index.php?title=National_Academy_of_Agrarian_Sciences_of_Ukraine&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Academy of Agrarian Sciences of Ukraine (page does not exist)"), [National Academy of Legal Sciences of Ukraine](/w/index.php?title=National_Academy_of_Legal_Sciences_of_Ukraine&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Academy of Legal Sciences of Ukraine (page does not exist)"), [National Academy of Pedagogical Sciences of Ukraine](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Pedagogical_Sciences_of_Ukraine "National Academy of Pedagogical Sciences of Ukraine"), [Minor Academy of Sciences of Ukraine](/wiki/Minor_Academy_of_Sciences_of_Ukraine "Minor Academy of Sciences of Ukraine").
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom_%281-2%29.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom_%281-2%29.svg.png) [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"): the [Royal Society](/wiki/Royal_Society "Royal Society") is recognised as the national academy for the sciences, the [British Academy](/wiki/British_Academy "British Academy") for the social sciences and the humanities, the [Royal Academy of Engineering](/wiki/Royal_Academy_of_Engineering "Royal Academy of Engineering") for engineering, and the [Academy of Medical Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Medical_Sciences "Academy of Medical Sciences") for medicine; the [UK Young Academy](/wiki/UK_Young_Academy "UK Young Academy") is for young scientists.
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/a4/Flag_of_the_United_States.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_States.svg.png) [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"): The [National Academies of the United States](/wiki/National_Academies_(United_States) "National Academies (United States)") include four organisations: the [National Academy of Sciences](/wiki/United_States_National_Academy_of_Sciences "United States National Academy of Sciences") (NAS), the [National Academy of Engineering](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Engineering "National Academy of Engineering") (NAE), the [National Academy of Medicine](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Medicine "National Academy of Medicine") (NAM), and the [National Research Council](/wiki/United_States_National_Research_Council "United States National Research Council") (NRC). See also [American Academy of Arts and Sciences](/wiki/American_Academy_of_Arts_and_Sciences "American Academy of Arts and Sciences"), [American Academy of Arts and Letters](/wiki/American_Academy_of_Arts_and_Letters "American Academy of Arts and Letters"), [American Academy of Diplomacy](/wiki/American_Academy_of_Diplomacy "American Academy of Diplomacy"), [Academy of American Poets](/wiki/Academy_of_American_Poets "Academy of American Poets"), [National Academy of Design](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Design "National Academy of Design").
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b3/Flag_of_Vatican_City_%282023%E2%80%93present%29.svg/20px-Flag_of_Vatican_City_%282023%E2%80%93present%29.svg.png) [Vatican City](/wiki/Vatican_City "Vatican City"): [Pontifical Academy of Sciences](/wiki/Pontifical_Academy_of_Sciences "Pontifical Academy of Sciences")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Flag_of_Vietnam.svg/40px-Flag_of_Vietnam.svg.png) [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam"): [Vietnam Academy of Science and Technology](/wiki/Vietnam_Academy_of_Science_and_Technology "Vietnam Academy of Science and Technology") (VAST)

Within most countries, the unqualified phrase "National Academy" will normally refer to that country's academy. For example, within the United States, the plural phrase "National Academies" is widely understood to refer to the U.S. National Academies.
